description: Root Cause and Debug Hints priority rules for CWF. Rules are evaluated in order; the first matching rule overrides the default priority. Each priority list is ordered highest-priority first.

_condition_operators_reference._summary: All conditions in a rule must be satisfied (implicit AND). Three operators work on string fields; boolean flags use direct equality.

_rule_actions.override: Default. Full replacement of priority order; evaluation stops at first match.
_rule_actions.adjust_last: Moves 'targets' IPs to the end of the current order. All matching adjust rules are applied (in declaration order) after override rules. Composable with other rules.
_rule_actions.adjust_first: Moves 'targets' IPs to the front of the current order. Same evaluation semantics as adjust_last.

_string_operators.{field}_in: Value is in the provided list.        e.g. "top_origreq_in": ["PortIn", "PortOut"]
_string_operators.{field}_contains: Substring present anywhere in value.  e.g. "core_mcas_contains": "bank"

_string_fields: top_origreq  – Top OrigReq value (e.g. PortIn, Read, RdCur)
top_opcode   – Top OpCode value  (e.g. RdCur, WrPush)
core_mcas    – Core top MC DECODE value
io_mcas      – IO top MC DECODE value (e.g. 'MCE when MCIP bit is set (MSCOD=2)')
mem_mcas     – MEM top MC DECODE value
_boolean_fields: other_present      – true when Other is non-empty

rules.name: PortIn or PortOut nuisance
rules.description: When Top OrigReq is PortIn or PortOut and a CHA Hint is available, the CHA error is a nuisance side-effect of a Core failure. Demote CHA below Core so the Core failure is surfaced as the primary root cause.

rules.name: IO present without Other — deprioritize IO
rules.description: When IO fails are present but there are no Other errors, IO MCAs are likely secondary (nuisance) indicators rather than root cause. Deprioritize IO to the end of the priority order.

rules.name: MEM present without Other — deprioritize MEM
rules.description: When MEM fails are present but there are no Other errors, MEM MCAs are likely secondary (nuisance) indicators rather than root cause. Deprioritize MEM to the end of the priority order.

rules.name: IO and MEM both present skeleton
rules.description: TODO: When both IO and MEM hints are present, both should appear in Debug Hints. Tune priority as data becomes available.

rules.name: Demand Read or RFO — CHA is root cause
rules.description: CRd, DRd, RFO mean Core is waiting for CHA to fill a cache line. Core 3-strike is downstream. CHA is root cause.

rules.name: Writeback or Flush opcode with CHA and Core — Core is root cause
rules.description: Wbxxx, CLFlush, CLWB, LLCWBInv are Core-initiated operations. CHA error is a downstream side effect.

rules.name: Lock or SplitLock with CHA — Core is root cause
rules.description: Atomic lock operations are Core-initiated. CHA serialization error is a victim.

rules.name: Core-only fail — pure Core hardware defect
rules.description: Core MCAs without any CHA or MEM hint indicate a pure Core hardware defect (IFU, DCU, DTLB pipeline/cache errors).

rules.name: IO MCIP bit set — deprioritize IO
rules.description: When IO MCAs (MC DECODE) shows 'MCE when MCIP bit is set (MSCOD=2)', the IO error is a secondary victim of another failure (e.g. CHA or Core). Move IO to the last position so the actual root cause surfaces. This is an adjust_last rule — it composes on top of whichever override rule (PortIn, Core-only, etc.) already fired, leaving the relative ordering of all other IPs unchanged.
